findings.message: FilesystemProjectStore._collect_asset_references() only checks for 'asset_id', 'asset_ref', and 'asset_ids' keys in scene node properties. This is reasonable for current use cases, but the comment in the handoff mentions this as a risk. Consider documenting this limitation in the method's docstring to make it clear that future asset reference patterns may need to be added here.

FilesystemAssetRepository._resolve_asset_path() handles three asset ID formats but doesn't validate that the resulting path is within the allowed project/asset directories. While it does reject paths containing '..', it doesn't verify that the final path is actually under _projects_root. Consider adding a final validation that the resolved path starts with the expected project root to prevent any potential path traversal edge cases.

The manifest format version is hardcoded as FORMAT_VERSION = 1. This is fine for now, but consider adding a mechanism to handle format migrations when the version changes in the future. At minimum, document the migration strategy in the code comments.

FilesystemProjectStore._serialize_scene_node() and _deserialize_scene_node() handle nested scene node trees recursively. This is correct, but there's no protection against extremely deep trees that could cause stack overflow. While this is unlikely in practice, consider adding a depth limit or using an iterative approach for very large scene graphs.

The test cleanup functions (_cleanup_user_path) use recursive directory deletion. While this is appropriate for tests, consider adding a safety check to ensure we're only deleting paths under the test root directory to prevent accidental deletion of important files in case of bugs.

acceptance_criteria.project_manifest_format: PASS - Manifest format includes all required fields (project_id, title, description, template_id, owner_profile_id, created_at, updated_at, world_ids, asset_references) and uses format_version for future compatibility.
acceptance_criteria.filesystem_project_store_implemented: PASS - FilesystemProjectStore implements all ProjectStorePort methods (save_project, load_project, list_projects) with proper error handling and directory structure.
acceptance_criteria.filesystem_asset_repository_implemented: PASS - FilesystemAssetRepository implements all AssetRepositoryPort methods (store, load, exists) with support for multiple asset ID formats and path traversal protection.
acceptance_criteria.contract_tests_added: PASS - Two new adapter contract tests added with comprehensive coverage of success cases, error cases, and edge cases. Tests verify file system operations, JSON serialization, and data integrity.

checklist.hexagonal_boundaries: PASS - Both adapters extend RefCounted (not Node) and only depend on domain types (Project, World, SceneNode, GameRule) and Godot filesystem APIs. No engine-specific imports beyond what's necessary for file operations.
checklist.kid_safety_and_parental_controls: PASS - Asset repository includes path traversal protection by rejecting asset IDs containing '..'. Project store properly validates input and handles errors gracefully without exposing internal paths.
checklist.storage_layout: PASS - Storage layout is well-organized: projects/{project_id}/manifest.json, projects/{project_id}/worlds/{world_id}.json, projects/{project_id}/assets/. This structure supports the requirements for TASK-006 (event-sourced action log) and TASK-009 (template loading).
checklist.contract_tests: PASS - New adapter contract tests follow the established pattern and verify method existence, return types, file system operations, and data integrity. Test runner updated to include new tests.
checklist.cross_agent_review: This review constitutes the required cross-agent review for TASK-005.

compatibility_verification.template_loader_integration: COMPATIBLE - The manifest format and world serialization support all fields needed by TemplateLoader (template_id, world structure, scene nodes, game rules). Asset references collection will work with template-instantiated projects.
compatibility_verification.event_sourcing_future: COMPATIBLE - The storage layout and manifest format can be extended to support event sourcing (TASK-006) by adding event log files alongside the existing structure.
compatibility_verification.plugin_sdk_integration: COMPATIBLE - Asset repository's multiple ID format support aligns well with plugin SDK requirements for flexible asset referencing.

summary: TASK-005 is well-implemented with a clean filesystem storage design that supports the project and asset persistence requirements. The implementation follows hexagonal architecture principles, includes comprehensive contract tests, and provides a solid foundation for upcoming tasks. Five low-severity findings identify areas for potential improvement (documentation, validation, and edge case handling) but none are blocking issues. Overall: APPROVE. The findings should be addressed as part of ongoing maintenance but do not block merging TASK-005.
